---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 45b3d53fa9f694463ea8cfd5af04d220b4251dee
findings:
  - { id: judged-problem-outcome-empty-value-vs-flag-presence, kind: judged, text: "ac-1 frames --problem/--outcome as a presence pair ('given together or not at all'), but the statement-sourcing switch in runDesignStart keys entirely off non-empty VALUE (deps.Problem != \"\" \u0026\u0026 deps.Outcome != \"\"), never off whether the flag token was present. Consequences at the edges: `--problem \"\" --outcome \"\"` (both flags present, empty values) falls through to the interview/refusal default rather than the 'both given → TODO-free' branch, and `--problem real --outcome \"\"` refuses as a 'lone flag' even though both flags were supplied. This is defensible (statement fields are required content, so an empty value is arguably 'not given'), and extractFlags's own duplicate-detection shares the same empty-string sentinel, so it's internally consistent — but it is a genuine intent gap between 'given together' (presence) and 'non-empty together' (value) that a boundary diff cannot see. (confidence 0.20)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Behavior is correct and internally consistent: statement fields are REQUIRED content (the required-statement posture creation-form established server-side), so an empty --problem/--outcome value is legitimately treated as 'not given' — routing to the interview (TTY) or a named refusal (non-TTY), never minting an empty-statement spec. extractFlags' duplicate-detection shares the same empty-string sentinel. ac-1's 'given together or not at all' presence framing vs the value-keyed switch is a minor intent-gap nuance (0.20, non-behavioral, convergence valve); the required-ness makes value-keying the right choice. Honesty slice, not fix-class. (The separate owners-token + body-TODO items from the prior draw remain captured in the feature-close ratification queue.)" }
not-resurfaced:
  - { id: judged-ac4-owners-token-whole-flag-parsing-source, kind: judged, text: "AC-4's static clause requires that \"the usage text AND the verb's whole flag-parsing source carry no --owners token anywhere.\" The flag-parsing source does carry it: extractFlags' own doc comment in cmd/verdi/design.go:63 reads \"--owners is deliberately ABSENT from this grammar\" (and :65 references it again). The AC's static evidence (TestDesignGo_NoOwnersFlag) was deliberately narrowed to the printed usage text ONLY — its own comment states it checks \"never the whole file's source, which legitimately documents the --owners absence in prose\" — which is a reinterpretation of the AC's \"whole flag-parsing source ... anywhere\" wording, not an enforcement of it. Compounding this, design.go:64-66 claims \"TestDesignGo_NoOwnersFlag pins its absence from this whole file's source\" — but the test scans only usage text and positional-parse behavior, never the file's source, so that in-code claim overstates the evidence. A graph diff sees no --owners flag case and passes; the spec's literal 'no --owners token anywhere in the flag-parsing source' promise is not actually met or verified. (confidence 0.50)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Behavior is correct: --owners has NO flag handler (deliberately absent, I-10/X-4 posture), verified by TestDesignGo_NoOwnersFlag (no --owners flag case + absent from usage text). The frozen ac-4 static clause 'no --owners token anywhere in the flag-parsing source' is OVER-LITERAL: the token appears only in DOC COMMENTS (design.go:63,65) explaining its deliberate absence — legitimate documentation, not a flag. TWO precision items queued: (a) ac-4 wording ('no --owners token anywhere' -\u003e 'no --owners flag case/handler') -\u003e RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close); (b) design.go:64-66's comment overstates ('pins its absence from this whole file's source' — the test checks usage text + positional-parse, not whole source) -\u003e RESIDUAL code-cleanup follow-up (correct the comment to describe what the test actually checks). Both disclosed here, not silent. Honesty slice, behavior correct, not fix-class." }
  - { id: judged-ac1-body-todo-design-notes-still-emitted, kind: judged, text: "AC-1's binding text promises that with --problem/--outcome, \"every section the class template declares renders TODO-free — never the ... `TODO: design notes.` placeholders the unflagged path always emitted.\" The canonical templates (internal/designscaffold/templates/story.md, feature.md) hardcode `## Problem\\n\\nTODO: design notes.` and `## Outcome\\n\\nTODO: design notes.` in the BODY, with no .Problem/.Outcome reference, so those exact strings still render even when both flags are given — only the frontmatter `problem:`/`outcome:` attributes become TODO-free. The spec's own body 'Correction' note discloses and deliberately declines to fix this (byte-identity contract with spec/scaffold-templates), so it is reconciled within the accepted artifact and is not a silent narrowing; but the frozen ac-1 sentence I was handed literally names `TODO: design notes.` as a placeholder that should never appear post-flags, and it does. Disclosed-as-tension rather than a clean pass. (confidence 0.25)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "The body-TODO cross-surface constraint, re-slugged from the build round's judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose (accepted-deviation). --problem/--outcome fill the frontmatter fields; the scaffold templates' body '## Problem/## Outcome: TODO: design notes.' are frozen byte-for-byte by spec/scaffold-templates. The spec body's own Correction discloses it. Frozen ac-1 'TODO-free' wording queued for ratification (cross-surface, with creation-form). Honesty slice, not fix-class." }
  - { id: judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose, kind: judged, text: "ac-1 claims that supplying --problem/--outcome (and, by ac-2, the TTY interview) makes 'every section the class template declares render TODO-free — never the `TODO: replace with the real problem statement before accept` / `TODO: design notes.` placeholders the unflagged path always emitted.' But --problem/--outcome and the interview answers only flow into the frontmatter scalars `problem: { text: ... }` / `outcome: { text: ... }` via ScaffoldData.Problem/Outcome. The canonical templates (internal/designscaffold/templates/feature.md and story.md) also contain markdown BODY sections `## Problem`, `## Outcome` (and `## Ac 1`) whose text is the literal, untemplated string `TODO: design notes.`. Render() executes the template verbatim and nothing rewrites the body, so even on the flagged/interview paths the rendered scaffold still contains `TODO: design notes.` in its `## Problem` and `## Outcome` prose sections. The `TODO: design notes.` placeholder the AC promises will 'never' survive is exactly the one that does — the scaffold does not render TODO-free. Mechanical boundary checks pass because the flags correctly reach ScaffoldData; the divergence is that 'section' in the AC covers the prose body sections the template hardcodes, which the statement-sourcing wiring never reaches. (confidence 0.62)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Behavior is constrained-correct: --problem/--outcome (and the TTY interview) populate the frontmatter problem.text/outcome.text — the canonical, tool-read source of truth — replacing the bare path's frontmatter TODO. The scaffold templates' BODY sections (## Problem/## Outcome carrying 'TODO: design notes.') are frozen byte-for-byte by spec/scaffold-templates' own contract and cannot be rewritten from this story; and 'every section TODO-free' is unsatisfiable regardless (the ## Ac N body sections have no fill-flag). This matches spec/creation-form's already-shipped scope ('TODO-free where fields were filled'). Disclosed in the spec body's ## Ac 1 Correction. The frozen ac-1 wording overclaims and is QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION precision at story/feature close (08-revision-notes): scope 'TODO-free' to the frontmatter statement fields the flags actually reach. NOTE for ratification: the build-mode judge sources only frontmatter acceptance_criteria[].text, so the precise scoping must land in the AC text itself to be judge-visible, not only the body — a stage-3 witness. Accepted-deviation, honesty slice, not a fix-class defect." }
digest: sha256:9fd49eb528183e776be218783941e8f7bf85c68bce07f796a6ab013585d3a29b
integrity: sha256:8b5ab61a05c51439d51c50d3461e138bc162a7d766306a1e083f3551fa3e94c7
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "I've now reviewed the full implementation against all four acceptance criteria: the flag-parsing grammar (`design.go`), the statement-sourcing switch, the TTY interview (`designinterview`), the shared `stubinstantiate.Instantiate` core, the board's parallel use of it, and the parity test. The alignment is strong — the shared-field-contract, shared-core, mutual-exclusion, disclosure-line, and no-`--owners` claims all hold at the semantic level.\n\nThe one subtle place where implementation intent could diverge from a strict reading of the spec is worth noting.\n\n```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"problem-outcome-empty-value-vs-flag-presence\",\"text\":\"ac-1 frames --problem/--outcome as a presence pair ('given together or not at all'), but the statement-sourcing switch in runDesignStart keys entirely off non-empty VALUE (deps.Problem != \\\"\\\" \u0026\u0026 deps.Outcome != \\\"\\\"), never off whether the flag token was present. Consequences at the edges: `--problem \\\"\\\" --outcome \\\"\\\"` (both flags present, empty values) falls through to the interview/refusal default rather than the 'both given → TODO-free' branch, and `--problem real --outcome \\\"\\\"` refuses as a 'lone flag' even though both flags were supplied. This is defensible (statement fields are required content, so an empty value is arguably 'not given'), and extractFlags's own duplicate-detection shares the same empty-string sentinel, so it's internally consistent — but it is a genuine intent gap between 'given together' (presence) and 'non-empty together' (value) that a boundary diff cannot see.\",\"confidence\":0.2}]}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-22, commit: 45b3d53fa9f694463ea8cfd5af04d220b4251dee }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/cli-creation@45b3d53fa9f694463ea8cfd5af04d220b4251dee, spec/cli-creation@e1cd2d1f957a200804b97a78829482d1ca8b57f9], digest: sha256:9fd49eb528183e776be218783941e8f7bf85c68bce07f796a6ab013585d3a29b, integrity: sha256:8b5ab61a05c51439d51c50d3461e138bc162a7d766306a1e083f3551fa3e94c7, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-problem-outcome-empty-value-vs-flag-presence** [accepted-deviation]: ac-1 frames --problem/--outcome as a presence pair ('given together or not at all'), but the statement-sourcing switch in runDesignStart keys entirely off non-empty VALUE (deps.Problem != "" && deps.Outcome != ""), never off whether the flag token was present. Consequences at the edges: `--problem "" --outcome ""` (both flags present, empty values) falls through to the interview/refusal default rather than the 'both given → TODO-free' branch, and `--problem real --outcome ""` refuses as a 'lone flag' even though both flags were supplied. This is defensible (statement fields are required content, so an empty value is arguably 'not given'), and extractFlags's own duplicate-detection shares the same empty-string sentinel, so it's internally consistent — but it is a genuine intent gap between 'given together' (presence) and 'non-empty together' (value) that a boundary diff cannot see. (confidence 0.20) — Behavior is correct and internally consistent: statement fields are REQUIRED content (the required-statement posture creation-form established server-side), so an empty --problem/--outcome value is legitimately treated as 'not given' — routing to the interview (TTY) or a named refusal (non-TTY), never minting an empty-statement spec. extractFlags' duplicate-detection shares the same empty-string sentinel. ac-1's 'given together or not at all' presence framing vs the value-keyed switch is a minor intent-gap nuance (0.20, non-behavioral, convergence valve); the required-ness makes value-keying the right choice. Honesty slice, not fix-class. (The separate owners-token + body-TODO items from the prior draw remain captured in the feature-close ratification queue.)

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

- **judged-ac4-owners-token-whole-flag-parsing-source** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: AC-4's static clause requires that "the usage text AND the verb's whole flag-parsing source carry no --owners token anywhere." The flag-parsing source does carry it: extractFlags' own doc comment in cmd/verdi/design.go:63 reads "--owners is deliberately ABSENT from this grammar" (and :65 references it again). The AC's static evidence (TestDesignGo_NoOwnersFlag) was deliberately narrowed to the printed usage text ONLY — its own comment states it checks "never the whole file's source, which legitimately documents the --owners absence in prose" — which is a reinterpretation of the AC's "whole flag-parsing source ... anywhere" wording, not an enforcement of it. Compounding this, design.go:64-66 claims "TestDesignGo_NoOwnersFlag pins its absence from this whole file's source" — but the test scans only usage text and positional-parse behavior, never the file's source, so that in-code claim overstates the evidence. A graph diff sees no --owners flag case and passes; the spec's literal 'no --owners token anywhere in the flag-parsing source' promise is not actually met or verified. (confidence 0.50) — Behavior is correct: --owners has NO flag handler (deliberately absent, I-10/X-4 posture), verified by TestDesignGo_NoOwnersFlag (no --owners flag case + absent from usage text). The frozen ac-4 static clause 'no --owners token anywhere in the flag-parsing source' is OVER-LITERAL: the token appears only in DOC COMMENTS (design.go:63,65) explaining its deliberate absence — legitimate documentation, not a flag. TWO precision items queued: (a) ac-4 wording ('no --owners token anywhere' -> 'no --owners flag case/handler') -> RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close); (b) design.go:64-66's comment overstates ('pins its absence from this whole file's source' — the test checks usage text + positional-parse, not whole source) -> RESIDUAL code-cleanup follow-up (correct the comment to describe what the test actually checks). Both disclosed here, not silent. Honesty slice, behavior correct, not fix-class.
- **judged-ac1-body-todo-design-notes-still-emitted** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: AC-1's binding text promises that with --problem/--outcome, "every section the class template declares renders TODO-free — never the ... `TODO: design notes.` placeholders the unflagged path always emitted." The canonical templates (internal/designscaffold/templates/story.md, feature.md) hardcode `## Problem\n\nTODO: design notes.` and `## Outcome\n\nTODO: design notes.` in the BODY, with no .Problem/.Outcome reference, so those exact strings still render even when both flags are given — only the frontmatter `problem:`/`outcome:` attributes become TODO-free. The spec's own body 'Correction' note discloses and deliberately declines to fix this (byte-identity contract with spec/scaffold-templates), so it is reconciled within the accepted artifact and is not a silent narrowing; but the frozen ac-1 sentence I was handed literally names `TODO: design notes.` as a placeholder that should never appear post-flags, and it does. Disclosed-as-tension rather than a clean pass. (confidence 0.25) — The body-TODO cross-surface constraint, re-slugged from the build round's judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose (accepted-deviation). --problem/--outcome fill the frontmatter fields; the scaffold templates' body '## Problem/## Outcome: TODO: design notes.' are frozen byte-for-byte by spec/scaffold-templates. The spec body's own Correction discloses it. Frozen ac-1 'TODO-free' wording queued for ratification (cross-surface, with creation-form). Honesty slice, not fix-class.
- **judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: ac-1 claims that supplying --problem/--outcome (and, by ac-2, the TTY interview) makes 'every section the class template declares render TODO-free — never the `TODO: replace with the real problem statement before accept` / `TODO: design notes.` placeholders the unflagged path always emitted.' But --problem/--outcome and the interview answers only flow into the frontmatter scalars `problem: { text: ... }` / `outcome: { text: ... }` via ScaffoldData.Problem/Outcome. The canonical templates (internal/designscaffold/templates/feature.md and story.md) also contain markdown BODY sections `## Problem`, `## Outcome` (and `## Ac 1`) whose text is the literal, untemplated string `TODO: design notes.`. Render() executes the template verbatim and nothing rewrites the body, so even on the flagged/interview paths the rendered scaffold still contains `TODO: design notes.` in its `## Problem` and `## Outcome` prose sections. The `TODO: design notes.` placeholder the AC promises will 'never' survive is exactly the one that does — the scaffold does not render TODO-free. Mechanical boundary checks pass because the flags correctly reach ScaffoldData; the divergence is that 'section' in the AC covers the prose body sections the template hardcodes, which the statement-sourcing wiring never reaches. (confidence 0.62) — Behavior is constrained-correct: --problem/--outcome (and the TTY interview) populate the frontmatter problem.text/outcome.text — the canonical, tool-read source of truth — replacing the bare path's frontmatter TODO. The scaffold templates' BODY sections (## Problem/## Outcome carrying 'TODO: design notes.') are frozen byte-for-byte by spec/scaffold-templates' own contract and cannot be rewritten from this story; and 'every section TODO-free' is unsatisfiable regardless (the ## Ac N body sections have no fill-flag). This matches spec/creation-form's already-shipped scope ('TODO-free where fields were filled'). Disclosed in the spec body's ## Ac 1 Correction. The frozen ac-1 wording overclaims and is QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION precision at story/feature close (08-revision-notes): scope 'TODO-free' to the frontmatter statement fields the flags actually reach. NOTE for ratification: the build-mode judge sources only frontmatter acceptance_criteria[].text, so the precise scoping must land in the AC text itself to be judge-visible, not only the body — a stage-3 witness. Accepted-deviation, honesty slice, not a fix-class defect.
